contraventionDescription: Parked in a taxi rank. Only taxis may park within a taxi rank – Contravention Code 45

sections.heading: How to have your penalty charge notice (PCN) for this parking contravention cancelled.
sections.content: We may be able to assist you in having your penalty charge notice for this contravention cancelled.
sections.contentHtml: We may be able to assist you in having your penalty charge notice for this contravention cancelled.

sections.heading: Follow our 3 stage process:
sections.content:
1 Check: Specific grounds for appeal for this contravention (below on this page)
This may also include diagrams and pictures showing signage requirements etc.
2 Check: General grounds for appeal
Applies to most contraventions.
3 Check: Summarised parking adjudicators decisions
Includes details of example cases won.
sections.contentHtml:
1 Check: Specific grounds for appeal for this contravention (below on this page)
This may also include diagrams and pictures showing signage requirements etc.

2 Check: General grounds for appeal
Applies to most contraventions.

3 Check: Summarised parking adjudicators decisions
Includes details of example cases won.

sections.heading: Specific grounds of appeal for this contravention
sections.content:
1. Was the bay marked correctly, a Taxi rank must be marked by a broken yellow line around the bay. There is no maximum length but the bay must be between 1800mm and 2700mm wide in order to comply with the regulations. The legend “Taxis” must appear along the length of the bay and on both sides where the bay is situated in the middle of a road. The legend “taxis” should be repeated at a maximum of every 12 meters. Please see information and diagrams below.
Below we show diagrams of the signs and bay markings which must be in situ by law and much more information that will help you decide whether to appeal against your penalty charge notice.
Please see our section on parking adjudicators decisions which will have details of cases for this contravention.
2. Where stopping by other vehicles is prohibited there must be a single yellow line of at least 200mm (this is much wider than a normal yellow line) and a “no stopping sign” with the legend of a purple shaded circle with a red outline and a red cross in the middle. The sign is shown in diagram 650.1 below.
3. Where waiting by other road vehicles is prohibited then there must be a sign stating “no waiting” and a legend of a purple shaded circle with a red outline and a red diagonal line .There must also be a double yellow line at the kerbside if no waiting is allowed at any time or a single yellow line if waiting is prohibited for specific periods. If there is no roadside signage and a single yellow line then the taxi rank restrictions only apply during the normal controlled hours and you can stop to drop/collect passengers at any time.
4. If there is no other signage present at the roadside adjacent to the taxi bay and the bay is marked by a normal width single yellow line is there a sign stating the enforcement hours of the single yellow line. If not the bay may be unenforceable unless you are in a Controlled Parking Zone (CPZ) in which case there should be  controlled zone signs on both sides of the road at the entry point of the controlled zone. If so were they facing the right direction?  Were they clearly visible from your car.?(ie. Not obstructed, defaced etc.).   If not, unless there is clear roadside signage within a maximum of 15 metres (3 car lengths) of where you actually parked your PCN should be cancelled as the contravention did not occur. London authorities such as Westminster (which issues more tickets than any other local authority) do not in many instances sign post their controlled parking zones correctly.
5. We have seen taxi ranks in Westminster in central London which operate from 6.30 PM – 8.30 AM.  During the day they are pay and display bays but at 6.30 pm they suddenly become a taxi rank ! Motorists are caught out as they leave their vehicles there and receive a PCN. If you receive a PCN check carefully the signs as when we looked the pay and display signs clearly stated that payment is required between 8.30 am and 6.30 pm “all other times and Sundays and bank holidays no charge” In this scenario your PCN would likely be cancelled if it was issued outside of the controlled hours.
6. Please remember that you may cite compassionate grounds  when making a representation to a local authority.
Below: Signs that should be in situ at a taxi rank. Please note the permitted variations and any sizes shown.

 

 
Below: Bay markings which should be in place on the road. Again check the minimum size of the bay and the other details.
Back to Parking
